en.extract:
The lesser hoopoe-lark (Alaemon hamertoni) is a species of lark in the family Alaudidae. It is endemic to Somalia where its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical dry lowland grassland.


Taxonomy and systematics
The lesser hoopoe-lark has alternately been named Witherby's lark.


Subspecies
Three subspecies are recognized:

Warangeli lesser hoopoe-lark (A. h. alter) - Witherby, 1905: Found in northern and north-eastern Somalia
Burao lesser hoopoe-lark (A. h. tertius) - Clarke, S, 1919: Found in north-western Somalia
A. h. hamertoni - Witherby, 1905: Found in central Somalia


References

The '''lesser hoopoe-lark''' ('''''Alaemon hamertoni''''') is a species of [[lark]] in the family [[Alaudidae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to Somalia where its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical dry lowland [[grassland]].

==Taxonomy and systematics==
The lesser hoopoe-lark has alternately been named '''Witherby's lark'''.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://avibase.bsc-eoc.org/species.jsp?avibaseid=8A913C34C1641D91|title=Alaemon hamertoni - Avibase|website=avibase.bsc-eoc.org|access-date=2016-12-09}}</ref>

=== Subspecies ===
Three [[subspecies]] are recognized:<ref>{{Cite journal|title=IOC World Bird List 6.4|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/ioc-lists/crossref|journal=IOC World Bird List Datasets|doi=10.14344/ioc.ml.6.4|doi-access=free}}</ref>
* '''Warangeli lesser hoopoe-lark''' (''A. h. alter'') - <small>[[Harry Forbes Witherby|Witherby]], 1905</small>: Found in northern and north-eastern Somalia
* '''Burao lesser hoopoe-lark''' (''A. h. tertius'') - <small>[[Stephenson Robert Clarke|Clarke, S]], 1919</small>: Found in north-western Somalia
* ''A. h. hamertoni'' - <small>Witherby, 1905</small>: Found in central Somalia
